open_questions: Stance context **watch** at **$222.65**; Lawrence base **32.0%** exceeds accumulate hurdle but human capital authority not set.
Component proof sum **$656/sh** base vs price **~$223**; Firefly option and AI reserve are widest judgment bands.
No third-party sources indexed; external blend unavailable.
Insider conviction score **2.73** (weak); net insider selling in past year (context only).
thesis: Adobe Inc. sells creative, document, and marketing software primarily through annual and monthly subscriptions. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$23.77 billion** (+10.5% versus **$21.51 billion** in FY2024), with **$22.90 billion** from subscription revenue. **[Fact]** Three reportable segments are Digital Media (Creative Cloud and Document Cloud), Digital Experience (enterprise marketing and analytics), and Publishing (legacy products). **[Fact]** The company reports a single operating segment for management purposes but discloses segment revenue and gross margin in the annual filing. **[Fact]** Customers are creative professionals, enterprises, and knowledge workers worldwide; revenue is diversified across Americas, EMEA, and Asia-Pacific.
why_market_wrong: The market may be treating Adobe as a mature, slow-growth desktop software vendor while subscription recurring revenue and generative-AI product cycles are still compounding. **[Fact]** Remaining performance obligation (RPO) was **$22.52 billion** at FY2025 year-end, with **65%** expected to convert to revenue within twelve months. **[Inference]** Generalists may overweight competitive fear from low-cost generative-AI image tools and underweight Adobe's installed base, workflow integration, and enterprise contracts. At **$222.65**, the stock trades near **9.6%** free cash flow yield on FY2025 owner cash, which may underprice a double-digit grower with **$22.52 billion** of contracted backlog.
